race.name: Gran Fondo Asheville
race.tagline: Serpentine roads, climbs, and thrilling Blue Ridge Parkway descents through the mountains near Asheville, NC

vitals.location: Asheville Yards Amphitheater, 75 Coxe Ave, Asheville, NC 28801
vitals.date_specific: July 19, 2026 (packet pickup); main event July 20, 2026 (subject to confirmation)
vitals.registration: Open; details via official app and website
vitals.feed_zones: 3 (Gran), 2 (Medio), 1 (Piccolo); stocked with water, sports drinks, gels, bars, fruit, snacks

climate.description: Mid-to-late July in Asheville: warm/summery (highs ~80-85°F/27-29°C), possible afternoon thunderstorms in mountains
challenges: Mid-to-late July in Asheville: warm/summery (highs ~80-85°F/27-29°C), possible afternoon thunderstorms in mountains

terrain.surface: Winding roads through pastoral countryside to mountain ridge vistas; Blue Ridge Parkway descents; well-marked with timed segments
features: Winding roads through pastoral countryside to mountain ridge vistas

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 74. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 71.

course_description.character: Downtown Asheville start → serpentine roads through countryside → mountain ridge climbs → Blue Ridge Parkway descent back to town. Timed segments (#2 Gran-only, #3/#4 multi-route). **Suffering zones:** mountain climbs, timed KOM segments[1][2][4][5]

logistics.transport: Start/finish at Asheville Yards Amphitheater. Packet pickup Sat 12-5pm, Sun 5:45am+. Staging by 7:45am, 8:00am start. Ride with GPS app required. Bib, bike plate, ankle timing chip mandatory. Right-side road for timed segments[1][2]

history.origin_story: Part of Gran Fondo National Series (America's original series). Hosted 2022 USA Cycling Gran Fondo National Championships. 2025 presented by Applewood Manor Foundation amid Asheville rebuild. 2026: July 19/20[2][4][5]

biased_opinion.summary: Classic National Series gran fondo with mountainous adventure, timed competition, and community focus. Strong format but lacks precise elevation/field data.

final_verdict.one_liner: Serpentine roads, climbs, and thrilling Blue Ridge Parkway descents through the mountains near Asheville, NC
